Petition For Order To Return Trust Property; For Order Compelling Accounting From Purported Attorney
Off Calendar Feb-24-2026 Continued To Mar-24-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together:
1) PTR-23-306594, The Mary L. McFadden Survivor's Trust; and
2) PTR-23-306697, The McFadden 1996 Trust.

Appearances:
Seth Skootsky and Robert Cross for and with Margaret and Anne McFadden.
Jaime Herren and Nils Rosenquest appeared for and with Patricia McFadden.
Sarah Brooks for Scott Hodges, who was also present.
Ellen Leonida for and with Monica McFadden.
Christopher Jew for Joseph McFadden, who was also present.
Mary C. McFadden appeared in pro per.

The matters were called for hearing on the pending petitions and for case management. The court discussed the status of the various petitions, anticipated discovery, and liquidity issues concerning the trusts, including payment of expenses and property sales. The court noted that a demurrer was set for February 26, 2026, and that the ruling would affect how the matters proceeded going forward. Ms. Brooks stated that she intended to withdraw the petition regarding payment of rent and instead file a new petition focused on requiring beneficiaries residing in trust properties to pay property-related expenses going forward, as outlined in a prior notice of proposed action.

Following discussion, the court authorized the sale of the remaining 50% interest in the real property located at 332 Jersey Street held by the Exemption Trust and directed Ms. Brooks to prepare the order. The court ordered the parties to work together to agree upon expenses to be paid forthwith to provide liquidity to the Survivor's Trust. The court continued the matters to March 24, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and directed the parties to submit a joint case management conference statement specifically setting forth the anticipated discovery each party expected to conduct and the length of time required for such discovery for purposes of trial setting.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court Reporter: Ellen Bellen, CSR 575, ellenbellen9575@gmail.com, 707-721-6228 (D204)

Petition For Order To Return Trust Property; For Order Compelling Accounting From Purported Attorney
Off Calendar Nov-26-2025 Continued To Feb-24-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were called together:
1) PTR-23-306594, The Mary L. McFadden Survivor's Trust; and
2) PTR-23-306697, The McFadden 1996 Trust.

Appearances:
Seth Skootsky and Robert Cross for Margaret and Anne McFadden, who were also present.
Jaime Herren for Patricia McFadden, who was also present.
Sarah Brooks for Scott Hodges, who was also present.
Ellen Leonida for Monica McFadden.
Amanda Sherwood for Joseph McFadden, who was also present.
Yulissa Zulaica for Mary McFadden, who was also present.
James McFadden and Leo McFadden were also in attendance.

The Court addressed several related petitions pending in both the Survivor's Trust and the 1996 Trust. Counsel disagreed on the appropriate sequencing of the disputes. During discussion, the Court noted that a recently filed amended petition for instructions had been submitted by Mr. Skootsky's office. The Court determined that the amendment to the petition must be property noticed in the normal course and that the pleadings remain open, and that trial setting cannot be addressed at this time.

The Court expressed concern about the substantial cost of continued litigation among nine siblings and several attorneys. After asking counsel to place their billing rates on the record, the Court strongly encouraged the beneficiaries to pursue mediation.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to February 24, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The related petition set for February 23, 2026 will be continued to the same date. The Court also set a Case Management Conference for February 24, 2026, and indicated that it will issue a case management order outlining a briefing schedule regarding trial information in advance of the next hearing.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Order To Return Trust Property; For Order Compelling Accounting From Purported Attorney
Off Calendar Jul-15-2025 Continued To Sep-30-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order To Return Trust Property; For Order Compelling Accounting From Purported Attorney
Off Calendar Mar-11-2025 Continued To Jul-15-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together:
PTR-23-306594, The Mary L. McFadden Survivor's Trust; and
PTR-23-306697, The McFadden 1996 Trust

Appearances:
Seth Skootsky for Margaret A. McFadden, Petitioner.
Jaime B. Herren for Patricia McFadden and Joseph McFadden, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Avid Alavi for Scott Hodges, Successor Trustee of the Survivor's Trust (via Zoom).
Mary McFadden, Interested Party in Pro Per.

The court noted receipt of the status reports and inquired about issues related to discovery, trust distributions, and mediation efforts. Mr. Skootsky argued that the exemption trust legally terminated upon the settlor's death in April 2023 and should have been distributed to its nine beneficiaries. He asserted that information about the trust's assets is crucial to mediation and that Joseph McFadden, as trustee, should provide full transparency. Ms. Herren agreed to voluntarily provide information about the exemption trust but noted that it is not formally before the court in this matter. She maintained that her client has been cooperative but reaffirmed that the exemption trust is not subject to the court's jurisdiction in this case.

The court addressed discovery propounded to beneficiaries who have not formally appeared as parties. Mr. Skootsky argued that many of the beneficiaries receiving discovery had submitted declarations and were active in the matter. Ms. Herren maintained that providing discovery did not constitute a waiver of their non-party status but stated that they had nonetheless agreed to provide requested documents.

The court raised concerns about how mediation expenses would be covered. Ms. Alavi reported that the Survivors' Trust holds approximately $116,000 in liquid assets but has significant liabilities, including deferred maintenance, property taxes, and insurance. She confirmed that the Survivors' Trust would cover mediation costs but noted that liquidity issues may require the sale of a property.

Mary C. McFadden expressed concerns about the prolonged nature of the dispute and stated that mediation should focus on full distribution of the estate rather than continued family conflicts. The court acknowledged her concerns and emphasized that all beneficiaries must participate in good faith.

The court continued the matter to July 15, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and set a hearing regarding status of mediation for the same date. Parties shall complete mediation by June 30, 2025. A joint status report as to the outcome of mediation shall be filed by July 8, 2025. The court shall issue a Breslin order outlining mediation participation requirements.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Order To Return Trust Property; For Order Compelling Accounting From Purported Attorney
Off Calendar Feb-11-2025 Continued To Mar-11-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together:
PTR-23-306594, The Mary L. McFadden Survivor's Trust, and
PTR-23-306697, The McFadden 1996 Trust

Appearances:
Seth Skootsky for Margaret A. McFadden, Petitioner.
Jaime Herren for Patricia McFadden and Joseph McFadden.
Mary McFadden, Amy McFadden, Joseph McFadden, Margaret A. McFadden, James McFadden, and Maureen McFadden were also in attendance.

Discussion was held regarding the potential need for the court to issue a Breslin order. Counsel agreed in principle to mediation but disagreed on timing. Mr. Skootsky cited outstanding discovery and missing financial information and suggested a 30-day continuance to allow time for counsel to meet and confer. Ms. Herren stated that 30 days was not enough time to prepare and suggested 120 days.

The court continued the matter to March 11, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Counsel was ordered to meet and confer in person or via Zoom (not by email or phone) by February 25, 2025, to discuss outstanding discovery requests and exchange necessary documents for mediation preparation. Counsel shall file a joint status report by March 4, 2025.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Order To Return Trust Property; For Order Compelling Accounting From Purported Attorney
Off Calendar Jun-26-2024 Continued To Sep-18-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-22-306594 The Mary L McFadden Survivor's Trust and
PTR-23-306697 The Mary L. McFadden 196 Trust.

Appearances: Jaime Herren appeared by zoom for and with Patricia McFadden (individually) by zoom and for and with Joseph McFadden (trustee of the exemption trust) in person.
Avid Alavi appeared by zoom for Scott Hodges (successor trustee of the survivor's trust).
Seth Skootsky appeared in person and Robert Cross appeared by zoom for and with Margaret McFadden in person and Ann McFadden in person.
Mary Catherine McFadden in propria persona.

The court stated that it saw the status report that was filed by Mrs. Herren and the objections to the status report that were filed by Mr. Skootsy. The court also stated that it is premature to be requesting attorney's fees, that a status report is not the appropriate way to request fees, that it will not set a briefing schedule today and noted that an evidentiary hearing is required on the issue of Margaret acting in bad faith. Mr. Skootsky stated that his clients greatly appreciate the appointment of Scott Hodges in the survivor's trust and commended Mr. Hodges for the work he has done with respect to the property ownership interests and the account that he located in the survivor's trust. Further discussion was held regarding the issue of requiring Mr. Hodges to be bonded for the amount of the liquid assets, the request for double damages, the parties being in the process of conducting discovery, it being premature to set an evidentiary, etc. The parties are to meet and confer regarding the bond issue. The court encouraged the parties to do private mediation. The Petition to Confirm Validity of Trust, etc., and the Petition for Instructions Concerning Ascertaining the Beneficiaries and Trustee, etc. are continued to September 18, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobol. Not reported.

Petition For Order To Return Trust Property; For Order Compelling Accounting From Purported Attorney-In-Fact
Off Calendar Apr-17-2024 Continued To Jun-26-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-23-306594 The Mary L. McFadden Survivor's Trust and
PTR-23-306697 The McFadden 1996 Trust.

Appearances: Seth Skootsky for and with Margaret McFadden and Anne McFadden (beneficiaries).
Amanda Sherwood for and with Joseph McFadden (trustee/beneficiary) and Patricia McFadden (beneficiary).
Avid Alavi appeared by zoom for and with Scott Hodges (temporary trustee of the Survivor's Trust) by zoom.
Mary McFadden, Monica McFadden and James McFadden (beneficiaries) were also present.

Discussion was held regarding the issue of having a briefing schedule, whether it makes sense to schedule an evidentiary hearing now or wait until certain matters are addressed, the irregularities in the 3rd and 4th amendments, whether the trust protector provision should be enforced, the delay due to figuring out where the monies that were withdrawn by Margaret went, etc. Mrs. Alavi reported that Mr. Hodges has marshalled a small Bank of America account with about $5,000.00, that she is unclear on whether the parties are requesting an accounting on that account, that there are nine pieces of property in which the two trust and the family members own fractional interests, that she has received the TIC and the property management statements for 2021, 2022 and 2023, that this is complicated and will take some effort, and that she sees no reason as to why all of the beneficiaries can't have access to all of the documents. Mrs. Alavi also stated that she intends to share all of the documents with counsel so that everyone can have transparency on the property management, that her client is working on the pieces of information, that they have filed notice of death on the properties and the child exclusion for the residence, and that amendment 3 and amendment 4 are very different distributions. Mrs. Sherwood stated that Joseph has been providing quarterly reports and requested that the funds be returned to Joseph so that expenditures and distributions can be paid. Mr. Skootsky and Mrs. Sherwood presented oral argument to support their positions with respect to whether or not it is appropriate for the funds to be returned to Joseph. Mr. Skootsky and Mrs. Sherwood are to provide proposed orders that reflect their requests/positions with regard to the return of property (said issue is under submission). The remainder of the Amended Petition for Order to Return Trust Property, etc., the Amended Petition for Instructions, etc., and the Petition to Confirm Validity of Truste, etc., are continued to June 26, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Guillory. Not reported.

Petition For Order To Return Trust Property; For Order Compelling Accounting From Purported Attorney-In-Fact
Off Calendar Feb-09-2024 Continued To Apr-17-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:

Jaime Herren, Esq., in court, for and with Patricia and Joseph McFadden, in court, Respondent/Trustee.

Seth Skootsky, Esq., and Bob Cross, Esq., in court, for and with Margaret McFadden and Ann McFadden, in court, Petitioners.

Mary Catherine McFadden, in court, in propria persona, Heir.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the subject matter. After additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Stephen Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Court Reporter: Nichole Jacobs, CSR: 13655; T: (707) 761-6596; E: nicholecsr13655@yahoo.com.
